lines.en_lit: to say that does not serve for the entire day decree lest eat Rav Adda bar Ahava said to Rava: And did Rabbi Yehuda issue a decree lest he eat? But didn’t we learn Rabbi Yehuda says: even designate another wife for him lest his wife die? And if his wife dies, performs service, and Rabbi Yehuda did not issue a decree lest he eat. said to him: How can be compared? There, since it is Yom Kippur, when everyone does not eat, he too not come to eat. here, when everyone eats, he too come to eat.
And like this, acute mourning take effect on him, she is divorced? Although acute mourning does not take effect on him, is he not troubled

MISHNA: all seven days he sprinkles the blood and he burns the incense, and he removes the lamps and he sacrifices the head and the leg On all the other days if wishes to sacrifice he sacrifices them, as the High Priest sacrifices portion first and takes portion first.
GEMARA: Who taught Rav Ḥisda said: not in accordance with Rabbi Akiva, as if Rabbi Akiva, Didn’t say: ritually pure upon whom a sprinkling fell, it renders him impure? how perform the service?

As it was taught “And the pure person will sprinkle it upon the impure person” on the ritually impure pure; but on the pure ritually impure. the statement of Rabbi Akiva. And the Rabbis say: These matters are stated only on items susceptible to impurity,

What is as we learned intended to sprinkle on an animal, but sprinkle upon person, if on the hyssop he repeat intended to sprinkle on a person but sprinkle upon an animal, if on the hyssop, he not repeat

What is the reason for of Rabbi Akiva? let the Merciful One write And the pure person will sprinkle it upon him, What Upon the impure person? Learn from it on the impure pure; but on the pure impure. And the Rabbis that It comes on items susceptible to impurity. However, here, a fortiori If on the impure pure; the pure all the more so not
And Rabbi Akiva That is what Solomon said: “I said I would become wise, but it eludes me” And the Rabbis The one who sprinkles and the one upon whom one sprinkles pure; but one who touches impure.

Is one who sprinkles pure? Isn’t it written: “He who sprinkles the purification waters will wash his clothes, What He who sprinkles? He who touches. But isn’t it written: He who sprinkles? And isn’t it written he who touches? And furthermore, one who sprinkles requires washing clothes, one who touches does not require washing clothes.
Rather, What He who sprinkles? one who carries But let the Merciful One write: One who carries; what is the reason he who sprinkles is written This teaches us must measure sprinkling.

This well to the one who said sprinkling requires a measure However, to the one who said sprinkling does not require a measure what can be said? Even to the one who said sprinkling does not require a measure that applies the back of the man; However, in the vessel measure is required, as we learned How much be in will be equivalent sprinkling? equivalent to dip
